EDCS-26801049.summary: This funerary inscription is a memorial erected by Iulia Fausta for her deceased husband, Iulius Saturninus, noting his age at death.

EDCS-26801166.translation: To Genius Nemesis. Iulius Germanus, decurion of the Ala Thracum, commander of the Cohortis II Sardorum. (career marker)
EDCS-26801166.summary: This inscription likely commemorates the career achievements of Iulius Germanus, a military officer, possibly on a milestone or honorary monument.

EDCS-26801199.text_edition: \n Caesareae quae introeuntes foeda indignaque nitore civitatis suae facie excipiebat consilio et instantia Publi Aeli Peregrini procuratoris eorum lapide stratam dignam congruentemque splendori patriae suae reddiderunt incohante illo ac dedicante\n
EDCS-26801199.translation: They made Caesarea, which used to greet those entering with a foul and unworthy aspect of its citizenship, paved with stone, worthy and appropriate to its civic splendor, through the plan and by the insistence of Publius Aelius Peregrinus, their procurator, who initiated and dedicated it.
EDCS-26801199.summary: This inscription records the paving of the city of Caesarea, initiated and dedicated by the procurator Publius Aelius Peregrinus, to improve its appearance.

EDCS-26801201.translation: To Iulia Augusta, mother of the emperors and of the camps. Gaius Iulius Turranicus, in honor of his aedileship, in the year 170 of the province.
EDCS-26801201.summary: This inscription dedicates an honor to Iulia Augusta, mother of the emperors, by Gaius Iulius Turranicus during his aedileship in year 170 of the province.

EDCS-26801379.translation: Terentia Cornelia lived 24 years. Dear to her family. She is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-26801379.summary: This is a simple epitaph for Terentia Cornelia, recording her age and expressing a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-27100082.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare Marco Aurelio [Commodo] Pio Felice Augusto tribunicia potestate patre patriae pontifice maximo burgum institutum per Titum Flavium Serenum procuratorem suum\n
EDCS-27100082.translation: In the reign of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Pius Felix Augustus, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, chief priest, a fort was established through his procurator Titus Flavius Serenus.
EDCS-27100082.summary: This inscription records the establishment of a fort under Emperor Commodus, supervised by his procurator Titus Flavius Serenus.

EDCS-27100083.text_edition: \n Ex auctoritate Imperatoris Caesaris divi Traiani Parthici filii divi Nervae nepotis Traiani Hadriani Augusti patris patriae pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate XXI consulis III proconsulis auspiciis Luci Aeli Caesaris Imperatoris IMP filii consulis termini positi inter Regienses et saltum Cu per Caium Petronium Celerem procuratorem Augusti anno provinciae LXXXXVIII\n
EDCS-27100083.translation: By authority of Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, father of the country, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 21st time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, under the auspices of Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of the Emperor, consul, boundary stones were placed between Regienses and the Cu pass by Gaius Petronius Celer, procurator of Augustus, in the 98th year of the province.
EDCS-27100083.summary: This inscription marks the placement of boundary stones between two communities, authorized by Emperor Hadrian and overseen by his procurator Gaius Petronius Celer in the 98th provincial year.

EDCS-27100085.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-27100085.translation: Under the Emperors Diocletian and Maximian Augustus, and Constantius and Maximian Nobilissimi Caesares, Gaius Julius Fortunatus, curator and administrator of the public affairs of the Albulans, restored the temple of the goddess Maura to its former condition, during the duumvirate of Gaius Julius Gaitatis the Younger and Lucius Seius Felix, the aedileship of Lucius Arrius Privatus, Gaius Mucius Mucianus the Younger, Aurelius [---], and Aurelius Quintus and Aemilius [---] presented from his own resources. Titus Flavius [Fortunatus] wrote this in the 260th year of the province.
EDCS-27100085.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to the goddess Maura in Alba by Gaius Julius Fortunatus, an official, during the reign of Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Maximian, in the 260th provincial year.

EDCS-27100139.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power, father of the country, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, grandson of the deified Pius Severus, son of the deified great Antoninus Pius, the order and the loyal veterans of the Altavenses, devoted to his divine power.
EDCS-27100139.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus by the local council and veterans of Altava, expressing their devotion.

EDCS-27100140.text_edition: \n timo recrorum amatori patriae ob votum sacris rerum sic prospere gestis Marcus Titius Castorius praefectus eius gratiam rettulit anno provinciae CCXVIII\n
EDCS-27100140.translation: To the most pious lover of his country, because of vows and because of the success of sacred matters, Marcus Titius Castorius, his prefect, returned thanks in the 218th year of the province.
EDCS-27100140.summary: This inscription records a prefect, Marcus Titius Castorius, giving thanks for successfully managed sacred affairs in the 218th provincial year.

EDCS-27100142.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Erenia Ianuaria lived for 95 years and died on the 7th day before the Ides of November in the 289th year of the province.
EDCS-27100142.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Erenia Ianuaria, who died in the 289th provincial year.

EDCS-27100149.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Crepereius Evassus lived for 50 years and died on the Kalends of May in the 272nd year of the province.
EDCS-27100149.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Lucius Crepereius Evassus on the Kalends of May in the 272nd provincial year.

EDCS-27100150.translation: [Perhaps: In memory of?] Remius Emeritus lived for 80 years and died on the 3rd day before the Ides of November in the 304th year of the province.
EDCS-27100150.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Remius Emeritus on the 3rd day before the Ides of November in the 304th provincial year.

EDCS-27100162.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Silvana lived for 23 years and died on the Nones of March in the 212th year of the province. [---]
EDCS-27100162.summary: This tombstone inscription records the death of Iulia Silvana on the Nones of March in the 212th provincial year.

EDCS-27100164.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. To Junius Secundus, dearest husband, who lived for 50 years and died in the 273rd year of the province.
EDCS-27100164.summary: This tombstone inscription is dedicated to the deceased husband, Junius Secundus, who died in the 273rd provincial year.

EDCS-27100167.translation: In memory of Lucius, who lived 70 years and died on the Kalends of November in the 294th year of the province; his sons made this for their dearest father.
EDCS-27100167.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius, set up by his sons, dating to the 294th year of the province.

EDCS-27100182.translation: In memory of Valeria Saturnina, their most loving mother, who lived 60 years and died on the 16th day before the Kalends of November in the 311th year of the province.
EDCS-27100182.summary: A funerary inscription for Valeria Saturnina, set up by her children, dating to the 311th year of the province.

EDCS-27100185.translation: In memory of Ulpia Florentina, who lived 23 years and died on the 6th day before the Nones of October in the 294th year of the province; her brother dedicated this to his dearest sister.
EDCS-27100185.summary: A funerary inscription for Ulpia Florentina, set up by her brother, dating to the 294th year of the province.

EDCS-27800203.translation: In memory, Victorina set up this for her sweet wife, who lived 40 years and died on the 3rd day before the Kalends of March in the 303rd year of the province.
EDCS-27800203.summary: A funerary inscription for Victorina's wife, set up by Victorina, dating to the 303rd year of the province.

EDCS-28100213.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly honoring the emperor Constantine.

EDCS-28100364.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Trajanus Decius Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, again high priest, with tribunician power II, consul II, proconsul IIII.
EDCS-28100364.summary: A dedication to Emperor Trajan Decius, listing his titles and offices.

EDCS-28200007.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus maximus Brittanicus maximus Germanicus maximus pater patriae tribunicia potestate XV consul III a Kaputtasacora milia passuum XX curante Quinto Munatio Celso viro egregio procuratore Augusti nostri\n
EDCS-28200007.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britain, conqueror of Germany, father of the country, with tribunician power XV, consul III, from Kaputtasacora for 20 miles; under the care of Quintus Munatius Celsus, an outstanding man, procurator of our Augustus.
EDCS-28200007.summary: A milestone inscription dating to the 15th tribunician power and 3rd consulship of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, set up by the procurator Quintus Munatius Celsus.

EDCS-28200017.text_edition: \n Imperatores Caesares [Marcus] [Opellius] Severus [Macrinus] Pius Felix Augustus et Marcus Antoninus [Diadu][menianus] miliaria constituerunt per Titum Aelium Decrianum procuratorem suum ad Syrorum numerum? milia passuum VIII\n
EDCS-28200017.translation: The Emperors Caesars Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus and Marcus Antoninus Diadumenianus established milestones through their procurator Titus Aelius Decrianus, 8 miles to the number of the Syrians.
EDCS-28200017.summary: A milestone inscription set up by Emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus through their procurator Titus Aelius Decrianus.

EDCS-28200080.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felice Augusto milliaria nova posuit per Publium Sallustium Sempronium Victorem procuratorem suum ab Altava mille passuum I\n
EDCS-28200080.translation: Under Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Publius Sallustius Sempronius Victor, his procurator, set up new milestones, 1 mile from Altava.
EDCS-28200080.summary: A milestone inscription set up by Emperor Maximinus through his procurator Publius Sallustius Sempronius Victor.

EDCS-28200082.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare Marco Iulio Philippo Invicto Pio Felice Augusto pontifice maximo tribuni<c=T>iae potestatis patre patriae miliaria nova posuit per Lucium Catillium Livianum procuratorem suum ab Altava Pomaria mille passuum I anno provinciae CCV \n
EDCS-28200082.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, high priest, with tribunician power, father of the country, Lucius Catillius Livianus, his procurator, set up new milestones, 1 mile from Pomaria, in the 205th year of the province.
EDCS-28200082.summary: A milestone inscription set up by Emperor Philip the Arab through his procurator Lucius Catillius Livianus, dating to the 205th year of the province.

EDCS-28200085.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felice Augusto et Caio Iulio Vero Maximo nobillissimo Caesare miliaria nova posuit per Quintum Valerium procuratorem suum ab Altava II\n
EDCS-28200085.translation: Under the reign of Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, the most noble Caesar, new milestones were placed under the administration of Quintus Valerius, his procurator, from Altava, 2 miles.
EDCS-28200085.summary: This is a milestone inscription dating to the reign of Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximus, recording the placement of new milestones.

EDCS-30100151.text_edition: \n Granius Liberalis miles cohortis II Sardorum vixit annis XXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-30100151.translation: Granius Liberalis, a soldier of the Second Cohort of Sardinians, lived for 35 years and is buried here.
EDCS-30100151.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Granius Liberalis, a soldier of the Second Cohort of Sardinians.

EDCS-30100152.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Faberia Saturnina vixit annis LXXXXV hic deposita\n
EDCS-30100152.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Faberia Saturnina lived for 95 years and is buried here.
EDCS-30100152.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death and burial of Faberia Saturnina.

EDCS-30100153.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sextus Umbrius Publi filius Quintianus vixit annos XXVIIII \n
EDCS-30100153.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Sextus Umbrius Quintianus, son of Publius, lived for 29 years.
EDCS-30100153.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Sextus Umbrius Quintianus.

EDCS-30100154.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Cassius Rogatus vixit annos LXXI\n
EDCS-30100154.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius Cassius Rogatus lived for 71 years.
EDCS-30100154.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Publius Cassius Rogatus.

EDCS-30100155.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Valeria.
EDCS-30100155.summary: This is a tombstone inscription fragment likely recording the death of Valeria.

EDCS-30501149.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Sempronius Faustus vixit annis XXXXV\n
EDCS-30501149.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Sempronius Faustus lived for 45 years.
EDCS-30501149.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Quintus Sempronius Faustus.

EDCS-30501150.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius ldiu Moderatus vixit annis LXXI hic situs est\n
EDCS-30501150.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Iulius Moderatus lived for 71 years and is buried here.
EDCS-30501150.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death and burial of Gaius Iulius Moderatus.

EDCS-30501151.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Claudius Africanus
EDCS-30501151.summary: This is a tombstone inscription fragment likely recording the death of Claudius Africanus.

EDCS-30501152.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucretia Quinti filia Mustia vixit annos XXII hic sita est\n
EDCS-30501152.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucretia Mustia, daughter of Quintus, lived for 22 years and is buried here.
EDCS-30501152.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death and burial of Lucretia Mustia.

EDCS-30501153.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeria Rogata vixit annis XXXX Valeriaetis e Optata Mascel vixit annis VII et Prisca vixit annis V\n
EDCS-30501153.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeria Rogata lived 40 years. Valeriaetis (daughter?) of Optata Mascel lived 7 years and Prisca lived 5 years.
EDCS-30501153.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Valeria Rogata and possibly her daughters Valeriaetis and Prisca.

EDCS-30501154.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucretia Rogata Lucreti filia vixit annis LXVII hic sita est\n
EDCS-30501154.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucretia Rogata, daughter of Lucretius, lived 67 years and is buried here.
EDCS-30501154.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucretia Rogata, daughter of Lucretius.

EDCS-30501155.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeria Rogata vixit annis \n
EDCS-30501155.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeria Rogata lived [X] years.
EDCS-30501155.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Valeria Rogata, though the number of years is missing.

EDCS-30501157.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tadia Satula Marci Tadi filia Arnensis vixit annis XXI hic sita est\n
EDCS-30501157.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tadia Satula, daughter of Marcus Tadius of the Arnensian tribe, lived 21 years and is buried here.
EDCS-30501157.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Tadia Satula, daughter of Marcus Tadius.

EDCS-30501158.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caecilia Marci filia Faustina vixit annos XXXV hic sita est\n
EDCS-30501158.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Faustina, daughter of Marcus, lived 35 years and is buried here.
EDCS-30501158.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caecilia Faustina, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-30501159.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Antonia Luci filia vata vixit annos XX\n
EDCS-30501159.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonia, daughter of Lucius, lived 20 years.
EDCS-30501159.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Antonia, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-30501160.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Aufustius Cai filius Arnensi Crescens vixit annis XXII hic situs est\n
EDCS-30501160.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aufustius Crescens, son of Caius, from the Arnensian tribe, lived 22 years and is buried here.
EDCS-30501160.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Aufustius Crescens, son of Caius.

EDCS-34200085.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum e Egregius SMN PVIC A IN L infer Tullia IC OV NR Ataxi praesidis provinciae Ponti OTAC VED divinam ornatorem CIE provinciae Mauretaniae\n
EDCS-34200085.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed, [in honor of?] Egregius... Inferullia, advisor of the governor of the province of Pontus, and Divina Ornatrix of the province of Mauretania...
EDCS-34200085.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or honorific text, possibly mentioning officials or individuals associated with the provinces of Pontus and Mauretania.

EDCS-43400004.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Flavia Julia Victorina, daughter of an Equites, who lived 2 years, 6 months, and 20 days. Died on the 10th day before the Kalends of December. [Buried in?] grave 185.
EDCS-43400004.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Flavia Julia Victorina, the daughter of an Equites, noting her age and date of death.

EDCS-43400838.translation: Lollius Simone lived thirty-two years. He died on the 11th day before the Kalends of December. [Buried in?] grave 275.
EDCS-43400838.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Lollius Simone, noting his age and date of death.

EDCS-43700642.translation: Monument of Eitinus Donatus, who lived for 80 years, in the 256th year of the province.
EDCS-43700642.summary: This is a funerary monument recording the name and lifespan of Eitinus Donatus.

EDCS-44100006.translation: His sons Fortucius Proculus and the others dedicated this to Sergius Sulpicius Passenus, their most loving father, for his piety; he lived 62 years in the 267th year of the province.
EDCS-44100006.summary: This is a dedication by sons to their father, Sergius Sulpicius Passenus, commemorating his life and their filial piety.

EDCS-44200055.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Septimius Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan and deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul, [erected by the] Rusuccuritanii.
EDCS-44200055.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), listing his imperial titles and lineage, erected by the Rusuccuritanii.

EDCS-44900076.translation: In these lands [belonging to] Marcus Cincius Hilarianus.
EDCS-44900076.summary: This inscription marks property belonging to Marcus Cincius Hilarianus.

EDCS-44900101.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. In memory of Lucius Victor, who lived more or less 85 years, in the 292nd year of the province.
EDCS-44900101.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Lucius Victor.

EDCS-44900119.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. I too am placed here, Puellaenilus Astuntius, son of Puellaenius Lucianus, who lived 7 years and 3 days and 7 hours. Pullaenius Lucianus [dedicated this] to his dearest and faithful son, in the 210th year of the province.
EDCS-44900119.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a young boy, Puellaenilus Astuntius, and is dedicated by his father, Pullaenius Lucianus.

EDCS-44900133.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Flaviosa made a cupola for Valerius Baribbalus, her sweetest husband, who lived 45 years, in the 261st year of the province and one [day?].
EDCS-44900133.summary: This funerary inscription records a wife's dedication of a sepulchre to her husband, Valerius Baribbalus.

EDCS-44900152.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Domitian Aurelianus, Pious, Fortunate, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-44900152.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Domitian Aurelianus, likely inscribed on a public monument, dating to his reign (c. 270-275 AD).

EDCS-46100489.translation: Memorial of Caius Domitius Claudianus, built from the ground and dedicated for the province in the 82nd year. Memorial of Caius Domitius, built from the ground and dedicated for the province in the 82nd year.
EDCS-46100489.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of a memorial, possibly for a public official named Caius Domitius Claudianus, dated to the 82nd year of an unspecified era.

EDCS-46200056.translation: Marcus Ulpius Martialis died, having fulfilled the office of duovir. He lived 40 years. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ulpius Ianurius lived 7 years, 5 months, and 10 days. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Matrona lived 3 years, 4 months, and 5 days. Caecilia Luciosa made this for her most rare husband and dearest sons. 200 units for the province, with the instigation of Ulpius Felix, the guardian, 1 silver uncia.
EDCS-46200056.summary: This is a funerary monument (or collection of funerary inscriptions) commemorating Marcus Ulpius Martialis, who held the office of duovir, and two children, Ulpius Ianurius and Ulpia Matrona, with a later addition by Caecilia Luciosa.

EDCS-46300055.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Julia Ianuaria lived 95 years; she rests here in the 213th year of the province.
EDCS-46300055.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julia Ianuaria, stating her age and the year of the province when she died.

EDCS-46300056.translation: Publius Sittius Ampelius: How good you were! Who would have thought that after your death your fame would be like this? Lived 26 years, in the 274th year of the province.
EDCS-46300056.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Sittius Ampelius, lamenting his early death and praising his reputation, with his age and the provincial year of death.

EDCS-46300181.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Aurelius tus miles legionis III Italicae Iuliae Domitiae coniugi inco<m=N>parabili bene merenti titulum posuit quae vixit annos XXVI menses V dies XX horas III mulieri posuit \n
EDCS-46300181.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Tus, a soldier of the III Italica legion, erected this monument to his incomparable and well-deserving wife, Iulia Domitia, who lived 26 years, 5 months, 20 days, and 3 hours.
EDCS-46300181.summary: A funerary monument erected by Marcus Aurelius Tus for his wife Iulia Domitia, detailing her age at death and his military affiliation.

EDCS-46300221.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Luciosia lived 25 years, and Aemilia Silvana lived 10 years, and Luciosia lived 10 years, and her son Datius lived 3 years. Aemilius Successus, the father, made this for his deserving wife and children. The 16th day before the Kalends of January in the 197th year of the province.
EDCS-46300221.summary: A family tomb inscription commemorating Claudia Luciosia, Aemilia Silvana, Luciosia, and Datius, erected by Aemilius Successus for his wife and children, dated to the 197th year of the province.

EDCS-46400039.translation: Of our lords. Emperor Caesar [[Marcus Iulius Philippus]] Augustus Invictus Pius Felix. 2 miles.
EDCS-46400039.summary: A milestone marking a distance of 2 miles, mentioning the emperor Philip the Arab and his titles, though the emperor's name is erased.

EDCS-46400166.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Augusto Vespasiano pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VI imperatori XII patri patriae consuli V designato VI censori coloni coloniae Iuliae Tubusuctitanae legionis VII immunis pecunia publica ponendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-46400166.translation: The colonists of the colony of Iulia Tubusuctitana of the VII legion, exempt from service, took care to erect this monument at public expense. To Emperor Caesar Augustus Vespasian, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 6th time, imperator for the 12th time, father of the fatherland, consul for the 5th time, and designated consul for the 6th time, censor.
EDCS-46400166.summary: A public dedication by the colonists of Tubusuctis to Emperor Vespasian, listing his numerous titles and honors.

EDCS-46400171.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Annius Tertullus, son of Lucius of the Arnensian tribe, lived 6 years; he rests here in the 175th year of the province.
EDCS-46400171.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Annius Tertullus, stating his age, tribal affiliation, and the provincial year of his death.

EDCS-46400222.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextia Secunda lived 50 years; she is buried here. Gaius Iulius Victor, her husband, placed this for his dearest wife. Dedicated in the 181st year of the province. To the spirits of the departed. Cilonius Misincius lived 63 years, in the 188th year of the province. Cilonius Secundus.
EDCS-46400222.summary: A tomb inscription recording the death of Sextia Secunda and Cilonius Misincius, with details of their ages, the provincial years of their deaths, and the dedications made by Gaius Iulius Victor and Cilonius Secundus.

EDCS-46400223.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Rodionius Donatus, son of Lucius Rodionius, lived 64 years. To the spirits of the departed. Furnia Prima, his wife, lived 60 years. To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Rodionius Felix, son of Lucius Rodionius, of the Arne(nsian?) tribe, lived 62 years. To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Rodionius Marcianus lived 53 years, in the 213th year of the province. The heirs made [this] in the 248th year of the province.
EDCS-46400223.summary: A large family tomb inscription detailing the deaths of Lucius Rodionius Donatus, Furnia Prima, Lucius Rodionius Felix, and Lucius Rodionius Marcianus, with their ages and provincial years, with the final construction dated to the 248th year of the province by the heirs.

EDCS-46400225.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Onoratus lived 45 years. He died on the 7th day before the Ides of November in the 283rd year of the province.
EDCS-46400225.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Onoratus, stating his age, date of death, and the provincial year.

EDCS-46700181.translation: A monument to Crescens, who lived 89 years.
EDCS-46700181.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of an individual named Crescens, who lived 89 years.

EDCS-47200093.translation: Made a table for Felix. Lived 2 years. In the 260th year of the province.
EDCS-47200093.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life and death of Felix, who lived 2 years, and notes the date by the provincial era.

EDCS-47200094.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Geminius Magnius lived 70 years and is buried here. February 26th. In the 175th year of the province. Gaius Geminius Magnianus dedicated [this] to his father.
EDCS-47200094.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Geminius Magnianus to his father, Gaius Geminius Magnius, recording his age and burial date, and the provincial year.

EDCS-47200339.translation: Table of Iulius Mascelus, who lived 61 years. In the 290th year of the province.
EDCS-47200339.summary: This inscription records the life of Iulius Mascelus, noting his age and the provincial year.

EDCS-47600556.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Tesilasen lived 70 years and died on the 20th day before the Kalends of August. In the 300th year.
EDCS-47600556.summary: A funerary inscription for Aurelius Tesilasen, recording his age at death and the date according to the provincial year.

EDCS-51000102.translation: To the god Sol Mithras, for the health of our lord the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Commodus Pius Felix Augustus. Aurelius ... with his family fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-51000102.summary: A dedication to the god Sol Mithras, made by Aurelius and his family for the health of Emperor Commodus, likely dating to his reign.

EDCS-53500011.translation: To the departed spirits. 'You whom piety led to honor parents, gladly we now receive: myself, I now take rest where Fortune sent me back, tris, old age; who would do such things unless love drove you? Greetings as you pass by: may all receive your honored parents.' 'If from the head you explore spirit and name, you will approve: Mummiclea Kamerina to her husband and Aelius Vitalis Kamerinus Sergianus to Vitalis his father and Saturnina his grandmother, most worthy, in the 266th year of the province, and Saturninus Vitalis [dedicated this].'
EDCS-53500011.summary: A complex funerary epitaph and poem, likely a dedication by Mummiclea Kamerina and her son Aelius Vitalis Kamerinus Sergianus to their deceased relatives, dated by the provincial era.

EDCS-54800035.translation: Lucius Clodius Dativus fulfilled his vow willingly and with good heart. In the 120th year of the province.
EDCS-54800035.summary: A votive inscription by Lucius Clodius Dativus, noting the fulfillment of a vow and dated by the provincial year.

EDCS-54800036.translation: Caius Iulius Fronto, priest, fulfilled his vow willingly and with good heart and dedicated [this]. May 7th. In the 233rd year of the province.
EDCS-54800036.summary: A votive dedication by Caius Iulius Fronto, a priest, on May 7th, dated by the provincial year.

EDCS-54800045.translation: ... sacerdos of the lord Saturn Sitifensis, together with Licinia Tertulla and ... Felice, son. In the 171st year of the province. In the 171st year of the province.
EDCS-54800045.summary: A dedication by a priest of Saturn Sitifensis, along with Licinia Tertulla and their son, dated twice to the 171st year of the province.

EDCS-58000061.translation: Tombstone of Caius Julius Felicianus and Rubia Novella, who lived [3] years, and of their sons Julius. Died on the 6th day before the Kalends of September in the 260th year of the province.
EDCS-58000061.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dating to the 260th year of the province (likely Roman Africa), commemorating Caius Julius Felicianus, Rubia Novella, and their sons.

EDCS-59600089.translation: Placed on the Kalends of June for the Good Goddess. Donatus the builder gladly fulfilled his vow in the 220th year of the province.
EDCS-59600089.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the Bona Dea by Donatus the builder in the 220th year of the province, likely on a public building or shrine.

EDCS-59700184.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filius divi Commodi frater divi Antonini Pii nepos divi Hadriani pronepos divi Traiani Parthici abnepos divi Nervae adnepos Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis VIIII imperator XI consul III consul IIII designatus proconsul pater patriae et Imperator Caesar Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepos divi Antonini Pii pronepos divi Hadriani abnepos divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepos Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus tribuniciae potestatis <IIII=III> consul designatus proconsul et Publius Septimius Geta Caesar Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filius Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti frater hiberna alae piae geminae Sebastenae pro pace in provincia constituerunt inchoante et dedicante viro egregio Publio Aelio Peregrino procuratore suo sic \n
EDCS-59700184.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus, son of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time designate, proconsul, father of the country; and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, grandson of the deified Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus, great-grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, and great-great-great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, holding tribunician power for the 4th time (corrected from 3rd), consul designate, proconsul; and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus, brother of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, established winter quarters for the loyal Gemini Sebastenian ala for peace in the province, under the supervision and dedication of the outstanding man Publius Aelius Peregrinus, his procurator.
EDCS-59700184.summary: This is a long imperial titulary inscription detailing the establishment of winter quarters for a cavalry unit by Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), and Geta, under the supervision of the procurator Publius Aelius Peregrinus.

EDCS-60200060.translation: To Caelestis Augusta. Gaius Julius Ingenuus gladly fulfilled his vow in the 211th year of the province.
EDCS-60200060.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Caelestis Augusta, dedicated by Gaius Julius Ingenuus in the 211th year of the province.

EDCS-60200062.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus [---] Donatus lived 41 years in the 220th year of the province.
EDCS-60200062.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Donatus, who died at the age of 41 in the 220th year of the province.

EDCS-60400044.translation: Gaius Julius Niceros, freedman of Iulius Iubas, a cook, lived 50 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-60400044.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Julius Niceros, a freedman and cook, who lived to be 50 years old.

EDCS-61800287.translation: Fortunata lived seventeen years. I beg you, if it is permissible, to lay aside such harsh griefs. Our flower was snatched away from her earliest years.
EDCS-61800287.summary: This funerary epitaph laments the death of Fortunata at the age of sixteen, describing her as a beautiful flower taken too soon.

EDCS-64800300.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Annius Simplex, son of Sedatus, lived 22 years in the 125th year of the province.
EDCS-64800300.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Annius Simplex, who died at the age of 22 in the 125th year of the province.

EDCS-65900122.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Escingus, a soldier, lived 75 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-65900122.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Escingus, a soldier who lived to be 75 years old.

EDCS-66300013.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus pater patriae consul divi Magni Antonini [filius] divi Severi [nepos] miliaria nova posuit per Titum Aelium Decrianum procuratorem suum r p p milia passuum XI\n
EDCS-66300013.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the country, consul, son of the deified Magnus Antoninus [---], grandson of the deified Severus [---], set up new milestones covering 11 Roman miles through Titus Aelius Decrianus, his procurator.
EDCS-66300013.summary: This inscription records Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Elagabalus) commissioning the placement of new milestones over 11 miles by his procurator Titus Aelius Decrianus.

EDCS-70000009.summary: This inscription appears to be a personal name, likely a tombstone or epitaph, of Lucius Caecilius Saevus.

EDCS-70000053.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus Folvio Felici veterano vixit annos LX Decineus frater eius pius fecit\n
EDCS-70000053.translation: To the Divine Shades. Folvius Felix, a veteran, lived 60 years. His devoted brother Decineus made this.
EDCS-70000053.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Folvius Felix, a veteran who lived 60 years, erected by his brother.

EDCS-70100004.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Caecilianus lived 40 years in the 130th year of the province.
EDCS-70100004.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Iulius Caecilianus, who died at age 40 in the 130th provincial year.

EDCS-70100006.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Iulius Macrinus lived 70 years. Lucilla, his daughter, made this for her well-deserving father with her own money in the 168th year of the province.
EDCS-70100006.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Iulius Macrinus, aged 70, and was paid for by his daughter Lucilla in the 168th provincial year.

EDCS-70100033.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Felix Faber, son of Iulius Felix, lived 30 years in the 257th year of the province. Ac(c)ia Urbana, his wife, made this.
EDCS-70100033.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulius Felix Faber, aged 30, in the 257th provincial year, erected by his wife.

EDCS-70100034.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [Text illegible]. LSI AIVMINIVLIN SMDINI lived 10 years in the 270th year of the province.
EDCS-70100034.summary: This funerary inscription, with a damaged text, records the life of an individual who lived 10 years in the 270th provincial year.

EDCS-70100035.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Paulus, his brother, made this funerary monument for his brother Marcus Iulius Bassus Simplicius with his own money on the 6th day before the Ides of November in the 260th year of the province.
EDCS-70100035.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by Paulus for his brother Marcus Iulius Bassus Simplicius in the 260th provincial year.

EDCS-72700004.translation: For the health of our lord Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus and his entire divine household, the soldiers of the III Augusta legion. [restorations]
EDCS-72700004.summary: This inscription is a dedication by soldiers of the III Augusta legion for the health and prosperity of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and his family, likely from the period of his reign (161-180 AD).

EDCS-73900433.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all the gods and goddesses, for the health, safety, and victories of our most holy Lord Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, and of Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta, wife of our Augustus, and of his entire divine household.
EDCS-73900433.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and other gods for the well-being and military success of Emperor Gordian III and his wife Tranquillina, likely from the 238-244 AD period.

EDCS-74100039.summary: This inscription records a command given by Peregrinus, identified with the deity Esus, possibly related to a local cult or administrative act.

EDCS-75500077.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, father of the country, and Marcus Opellius Antoninus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, Augusti, ordered milestones to be placed through their procurator, the distinguished man Claudius Pyrrhus, from Aras, 7 miles.
EDCS-75500077.summary: This inscription records the construction of milestones ordered by Emperors Macrinus and Antoninus (Caracalla) and overseen by their procurator Claudius Pyrrhus, dating to their joint rule.

EDCS-78300399.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Monument of [---]ialian, who lived 80 years.
EDCS-78300399.summary: This is a funerary monument recording the name and age at death of the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-79000128.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Domitius Germanus, incomparable decurion, father, who lived 65 years. Domitius Rogatus and Faustus, his sons, and Domitia Romula(?), [erected this] for Domitius Germanus.
EDCS-79000128.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by sons for their father, Domitius Germanus, a decurion, and is dated to the 297th year of the province.

EDCS-80600030.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the Roman Emperor Lucius Aurelius Verus, likely part of a larger dedicatory or honorific text.

EDCS-81200115.translation: Cuteus, a soldier of the equestrian rank of the 1st Cohort of Pannonians, Fisius, aged 3 years and 6 months.
EDCS-81200115.summary: This inscription records the death of a very young child, Fisius, who was the son of Cuteus, an equestrian soldier in the 1st Cohort of Pannonians.
